title: Child-rape sentence reignites scrutiny of UK high street mini-marts
description: Rapist Bawan Harwe lured underage girls back to his flat with the promise of free vapes and cash.

title: Dozens of children put at risk after gender care failures at GP clinic, inquiry finds
description: A safety inquiry into the WellBN clinic in Brighton found 78 children were potentially harmed after being given gender medication without proper checks.

title: El Niño under way and threatens weather extremes, scientists say
description: An El Niño event has officially started, say US scientists, raising fears of extreme weather and higher temperatures.

title: 'Mum was killing me': England's Declan Rice on sunburn for his World Cup photoshoot
description: The Arsenal midfielder admits he was a touch pink in pre-tournament photos as players try to adjust to scorching conditions.

title: The rise and rise of 'Meatball' McGinn
description: John McGinn was a boy from Clydebank with a "big, round head" and bigger dreams. Scotland's World Cup opener marks a new high in a storybook career.

title: Catch up on the World Cup without any spoilers
description: It is a dilemma plenty of fans in the UK will have - and we are giving you the chance to follow the World Cup without spoilers.

title: Find your local World Cup hero with our postcode lookup
description: Which World Cup stars have roots near you? Find out with our interactive lookup tool

title: Why defence funding deal offered to Healey wasn't enough to keep him
description: The defence secretary was pressing the prime minister for a larger increase in defence spending than he was offered, the BBC understands.

title: BBC tours UFC arena at White House
description: White House reporter Bernd Debusmann gets a preview of the temporary structure that will host a UFC fight 14 June.

title: Jilted bride who went ahead with wedding gets engaged
description: Rather than cancel the £12,000 big day, Kayley Stead went ahead with it, posting videos and photos online.

title: Three ships attacked by the US in three days: What we know
description: Three tankers have been struck by the US military over the past three days, killing at least three people.

title: 'I haven't left my house, I'm scared' says Mean Girls actor as Belfast shows cancelled over violence
description: The Mean Girls musical's run in Belfast is cancelled following disorder in the city.

title: Facing a seismic by-election, the people of Makerfield tell us what matters to them
description: In conversations with residents, business owners and campaigners in Makerfield, the BBC found a mixed mood.

title: Mis-Teeq on reuniting, UK garage and Alesha Dixon's ad libs
description: The noughties girl group are back for a reunion gig to celebrate the 25th anniversary of their debut album.

title: Weight-loss drug Wegovy to be available in pill form in UK for first time
description: Manufacturer Novo Nordisk says a daily tablet of the drug could be more convenient for some people than weekly injections.

title: Three jailed for violence at Henry Nowak police protest
description: Disorder broke out in Southampton close to where the 18-year-old student was murdered.

title: Asylum appeal backlog at record high, new figures show
description: While the government has cut the backlog of cases waiting for a decision, the number of appeals has risen.

title: Police officer, 19, dies from crash injuries
description: Jess Turnbull "had so much to look forward to in her career and her life", the chief constable says.

title: Ryanair investigated over charging parents to sit with children
description: The UK's competition regulator is investigating the airline over charges it imposes on parents to sit next to their child.

title: Counter-terrorism police leading school stabbing inquiry
description: Two pupils, both 14, and a teacher were hurt in the attack at a school in Manchester.
